abstract: Consumers curate collections of items for various reasons and categorize them into subsets or categories based on different criteria as their collections grow. The items in a collection reflect a consumer's preferences, and the categories provide insights into the different contexts in which items are consumed. The authors develop a novel deep generative modeling framework that captures the network structure of consumer collections using multiple interlocked hypergraphs. This model employs message-passing variational autoencoders that leverage hypergraph structures and entity-specific covariates to generate probabilistic deep embeddings for consumers, items, and item categories. Applying this framework to digital music collections and playlists of music consumers, the authors demonstrate that the model outperforms several sophisticated benchmarks in predicting linkages within these collections. They then illustrate how this approach enables firms to generate novel personalized product bundles, recommend relevant items and bundles, and dynamically expand existing bundles with new items. Beyond the music application, this method is broadly applicable to other consumer collections, such as food recipes and content collections on social curation platforms like Pinterest.

ob.ai_reason: 使用神经网络建模消费者收藏，AI为方法非议题
ob.tldr: 本研究针对消费者收藏的分类结构，提出基于互锁超图的深度生成模型，通过消息传递变分自编码器学习消费者、商品及类别的概率嵌入。在数字音乐收藏与播放列表上的实验表明，该模型在链接预测任务上优于多个先进基准，并能生成个性化商品捆绑、动态推荐和扩展现有捆绑。该框架适用于食谱、社交平台内容等多种收藏。

title: Modeling Categorized Consumer Collections with Interlocked Hypergraph Neural Networks
title_zh: 基于互锁超图神经网络的分类消费者集合建模
